title: Emperor's skyhook
content:
{{Top|fa|leg}} {{SpaceStation |image=[[File:EmperorsSkyhook-SOTETC.jpg]] |name=Emperor's skyhook |designer= |manufacturer= |line= |model= |type=[[Skyhook/Legends|Skyhook]][[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire (novel)|''Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire'' novel]] |cost= |modifier= |sysmods= |length= |width= |height= |diameter= |mass= |max accel= |mglt= |max speed= |maneuverability= |engine= |hyperdrive= |hdrange= |hdsystem= |poweroutput= |power= |shield gen= |hull= |sensor= |target= |navigation= |avionics= |maincomp= |countermeasures= |armament= |complement= |bays= |escapepods= |crew= |skeleton= |passengers= |capacity= |cargohandling= |consumables= |lifesupport= |communications= |othersystems= |region=[[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]]{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} |sector= |system=[[Coruscant system/Legends|Coruscant system]] |coordinates=L-9 |xyz= |orbited=[[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] |distance= |routes= |role= |affiliation=*[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] *[[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]''[[X-Wing: The Krytos Trap]]'' |constructed= |destroyed= |retired= |battles=[[Mission above Imperial City]] |owners=[[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] |namedcrew=[[Palpatine's gardener]] |captains= |registry= |aliases= |population= |interest=*Central park *[[Emperor's throne room]]''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Third Edition, Revised and Expanded]]'' {{C|"Emperor's throne room"}} *Security office |services= |sectors= |modules= }} {{Quote|Tell your master I will see him. I have business on the Emperor's skyhook. Have him meet me there in three standard hours.|Darth Vader, to [[Guri]]|}} The '''Emperor's skyhook''' was the extravagant personal [[Skyhook/Legends|skyhook]] retreat of [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] during his reign as supreme ruler of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. The skyhook hung in low geosynchronous [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]] above the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Capital/Legends|throneworld]] of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] and was tethered to the [[Planet/Legends|planet's]] surface in the [[Palace District]] of [[Galactic City/Legends|Imperial City]] via a long umbilical connection that provided [[Turbolift/Legends|turbolift]] access to the [[Space station/Legends|station]]. The [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor's]] skyhook, grandiose in size and carefully protected, boasted a sprawling central park with a magnificently tailored [[Garden/Legends|garden]] of massive [[Tree/Legends|timbers]] and a climate-controlled jungle that was maintained by the Emperor's best [[Palpatine's gardener|horticulturist]]. The [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] and [[Dark Prince]] [[Xizor/Legends|Xizor]] of the [[Black Sun/Legends|Black Sun]] crime syndicate met briefly on the Emperor's skyhook in [[3 ABY/Legends|3.5 ABY]], discussing the existence of a secret [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] [[Vergesso Base|shipyard]], intelligence of which Xizor offered to the Empire as a means of increasing his favor with the Emperor. In the meeting's aftermath, the Emperor [[Assassin/Legends|arranged]] the [[Death/Legends|death]] of his gardener after Xizor, greatly impressed with the man's botanical work, hired him away to work on the Dark [[Prince/Legends|Prince's]] own [[Falleen's Fist|personal skyhook]]. The [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] later gained control of the skyhook after [[Liberation of Coruscant (Galactic Civil War)|seizing Coruscant]] from the Empire and used the installation to track [[Palpatine Counter-Insurgency Front|enemy]] aerial traffic in Imperial City during a [[Mission above Imperial City|military operation]] in [[7 ABY/Legends|7 ABY]] before grounding all of Coruscant's skyhooks for safety concerns. ==Description== {{Quote|Find out who is in charge of this. Offer him twice the credits he is being paid to come to work on my skyhook.|Prince Xizor|}} [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] possessed a''[[Coruscant and the Core Worlds]]'' grandiose,{{CSWECite|I|253|''Falleen's Fist''}}''[[Star Wars: Behind the Magic]]''''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' {{C|"''Falleen's Fist''"}} opulent personal [[Skyhook/Legends|skyhook]], a [[City/Legends|city]]-sized [[Satellite/Legends|satellite]] [[Space station/Legends|installation]] that hung in low geosynchronous [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]] over the [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], above the [[Imperial Palace/Legends|Imperial Palace]] of [[Galactic City/Legends|Imperial City]].{{CSWECite|I|233|Emperor's skyhook}} The [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor's]] skyhook was tethered to Coruscant's surface by a long umbilical''[[Shadows of the Empire Sourcebook]]'' that provided [[Turbolift/Legends|turbolift]] access to the orbiting structure, by which most passengers and cargo traveled. Access to the skyhook via personal [[Transport/Legends|transport]] was alternatively available. By comparison, the Emperor's skyhook was one-and-a-half times as large as ''[[Falleen's Fist]]'', the palatial skyhook''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Third Edition, Revised and Expanded]]'' {{C|"''Falleen's Fist''"}} of [[Dark Prince]] [[Xizor/Legends|Xizor]], [[Underlord]] of the [[Black Sun/Legends|Black Sun]] crime syndicate, and far more luxurious. [[File:PalpatineGuards-NEGTC.png|thumb|left|200px|Emperor Palpatine, who owned the skyhook in orbit of Coruscant]] The Emperor kept some of his best treasures in his huge [[Realspace/Legends|space]] habitat. The skyhook's interior was dominated by an enormous central park encircled by a wide, elevated, lighted terrace that looked out over the plaza.{{Topps|set=1996 Topps Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire|cardname=Xizor Wants it All|link=https://www.rebelscum.com/photo.asp?image=/sote/soteTC021.jpg}} The park, a verdant botanical [[Garden/Legends|garden]], was maintained by a [[Palpatine's gardener|gardener]], the Emperor's best [[horticulturist]]. Standing at the middle of the central park was a giant, multi-tiered fountain from which [[Water/Legends|water]] cascaded into a series of large streams meandering throughout the garden. Flanking the fountain itself were two massive, identical [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] statues with [[Limb/Legends|arms]] raised, holding illuminated globes above their heads. The top of the fountain extended in a spire to the park's expansive, transparent ceiling, which offered a sparkling view of the surrounding [[Coruscant system/Legends|Coruscant system]]. The park's flora included full-grown [[evergreen]] and deciduous [[Tree/Legends|trees]], some topping thirty [[Meter/Legends|meters]] in height.''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' {{C|"Emperor's skyhook"}} One section of the park was climate-controlled into a thriving jungle, complete with [[Color/Legends|colorful]] and phosphorescent [[Flower/Legends|flowers]] of every hue, and other [[Plant/Legends|plants]] among them, including a waxy, broad-leafed vine that was naturally nearly black. The [[Oxygen/Legends|oxygen]]-rich jungle emanated a fine odor teeming with life, a combination of wet [[Mushroom/Legends|mushrooms]], leaf mold, and fresh [[Grass/Legends|grasses]]. A gentle chorus of [[Music/Legends|singing]] [[Bird/Legends|birds]] echoed throughout the park.[[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire (abridged audiobook)|''Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire'' abridged audiobook]] As with all [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] locations, the Emperor's skyhook maintained a special [[Emperor's throne room|throne room]] reserved for the Emperor's personal use, from where he could monitor and manage all Imperial activity and contemplate the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]]. The skyhook also maintained private [[Holocamera/Legends|holocameras]] that visually recorded activity aboard, and, by the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], the orbiting structure had a security office capable of monitoring aerial traffic from the planet below. ==History== ===Imperial grandeur=== {{Quote|I really must see about hiring your spies away from you. Especially after you stole my best horticulturist. A pity the man had that fatal lift accident before he could start working for you.''"
"''Yes, a pity.|Emperor Palpatine and Prince Xizor|}} During Emperor Palpatine's dominion of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], skyhooks became symbols of prestige and power in the skies over Imperial Center.{{CSWECite|III|160|skyhook}} Some of the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]]' most prominent citizens maintained skyhooks above Coruscant as exclusive luxury retreats. The Emperor himself, being no exception, enjoyed his own extravagant and well-protected personal skyhook. In the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[3 ABY/Legends|3.5 ABY]],''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' in the [[Standard month/Legends|months]] following the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]] during the height of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] tended to business on the Emperor's skyhook. He agreed beforehand to a requested meeting aboard the skyhook with Dark [[Prince/Legends|Prince]] Xizor, leader of the Black Sun criminal empire, who had carefully manipulated his way into the Emperor's favor in a personal bid for power. Rather than journey to the skyhook via conventional means through the station's turbolift tether, however, Vader opted to travel aboard his own [[ST 321/Legends|personal shuttle]]. He preferred to be in control of his own [[Lambda-class T-4a shuttle/Legends|ship]] and dark side [[Force power/Legends|mastery]] rather than placing his stock in a skyhook lift, which was vulnerable to internal and external ambush. [[File:Calculation_XWM.png|thumb|right|250px|Prince Xizor, who briefly met with Darth Vader on the Emperor's skyhook]] While waiting for Vader to arrive, Xizor, flanked by a dozen [[Bodyguard/Legends|bodyguards]], stood on a terrace balcony overlooking the sprawling central park of the Emperor's skyhook. Although Xizor cared little for botany, the grandeur and magnificence of the garden below greatly impressed him and heightened his appetite for power. The Dark Prince even considered hiring away the Emperor's gardener for his own personal skyhook. Once Vader did arrive, Xizor informed the Dark [[Lord/Legends|Lord]] of a sizable [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] [[Vergesso Base|shipyard]] operating in the distant [[Bajic sector/Legends|Bajic sector]], which, unknown to the Empire, was owned by [[Ororo Transportation/Legends|Ororo Transportation]], a rival of Black Sun. By orchestrating the [[Battle of the Bajic Shipyards|destruction]] of the Rebel base, Xizor planned to benefit twofold—his trust with the Emperor would increase, while a competitor would be greatly damaged. Once the meeting with Vader concluded, Xizor indeed instructed one of his [[Unidentified Black Sun bodyguard|guards]] to discover who was responsible for the Emperor's garden and to offer him twice his [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|salary]] to work on Xizor's own skyhook. Vader, wary of Xizor's growing influence with the Emperor, had the recordings of the meeting erased from the skyhook's holocameras and his own recordings sealed away, and did not reveal to the Emperor the source of the intelligence regarding the Rebel [[Shipyard/Legends|shipyard]]. Meanwhile, the Emperor's horticulturist accepted Xizor's offer, only to meet with a [[Assassin/Legends|fatal]] lift accident of the Emperor's design shortly thereafter. ===New Republic control=== {{Quote|Your father's getting us a tracking feed from the security office onboard the Emperor's skyhook, but there is a chance some might get away.|Commander Wedge Antilles, speaking of General Airen Cracken, briefs Rogue Squadron|}} Following the death of the Emperor and the Rebel Alliance's defeat of the Empire at the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], the New Republic—the Rebellion's successor state—[[Liberation of Coruscant (Galactic Civil War)|liberated Coruscant]] from Imperial rule. The New Republic eventually assumed control of the Emperor's skyhook, still in orbit of the galactic capital. In [[7 ABY/Legends|7 ABY]], the New Republic organized a [[Mission above Imperial City|mission]] to disrupt the [[Palpatine Counter-Insurgency Front]] (PCF), a pro-Imperial [[Terrorist/Legends|terrorist]] group that was preparing a strike against a [[Bacta/Legends|bacta]] storage facility in Coruscant's [[Alien Protection Zone/Legends|Alien Protection Zone]]. The New Republic [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] [[Squadron/Legends|unit]] [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]] would be tasked with destroying all enemy [[Airspeeder/Legends|speeder]] craft, any number of which the New Republic believed would be transporting a bomb. [[File:YsanneIsard-NEGTC.png|thumb|left|200px|Ysanne Isard, whose escape from Coruscant led to the New Republic Provisional Council grounding all skyhooks for safety reasons]] Because any PCF speeder craft would be more easily able to navigate the urban maze of Imperial City than Rogue Squadron's [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing starfighters]], [[Chief of Intelligence/Legends|Alliance Intelligence Chief]] [[General/Legends|General]] [[Airen Cracken/Legends|Airen Cracken]] arranged for the Emperor's skyhook to provide Rogue Squadron with a tracking feed from the skyhook's security office during the operation. Nevertheless, as squadron [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]] admitted pre-battle, some enemy craft might still slip away in the fighting. Ultimately, Rogue Squadron succeeded in stopping the terrorist group's attack, although the battle saw the escape of de facto Imperial leader [[Ysanne Isard]] from Coruscant aboard the [[Executor-class Star Dreadnought/Legends|Super Star Destroyer]] ''[[Lusankya/Legends|Lusankya]]''. In the wake of Isard's destructive flight from Coruscant, the [[New Republic Provisional Council]] grounded all skyhooks for safety reasons. The grounded skyhooks previously hanging over Imperial City's [[Palace District]] served as housing for survivors of the battle and allowed for the resources being used to maintain those skyhooks in orbit to go toward other endeavors.''[[X-Wing: The Bacta War]]'' ==Behind the scenes== [[File:ColorlessImperialSkyhooksHoverFuriously-AOTBH.png|thumb|right|275px|Original sketch drawing of the Emperor's skyhook for the ''Shadows of the Empire'' trading card set]] The Emperor's skyhook first appeared in the [[May]] [[1996]] novel [[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire (novel)|''Shadows of the Empire'']], written by [[Steve Perry]] as part of the larger [[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire|''Shadows of the Empire'']] multimedia project. The skyhook's interior central park, as described in the novel, was illustrated by artists [[Tim Hildebrandt]] and [[Greg Hildebrandt]] for the series' [[Topps]] [[1996 Topps Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire|''Shadows of the Empire'' trading card]] set. Choosing to depict the Emperor's skyhook allowed the Hildebrandt brothers an opportunity to paint a spectacular landscape for the set, which was one of their hallmarks as artists.''[[Star Wars: The Art of the Brothers Hildebrandt]]'' ==Appearances== *[[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire (novel)|''Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire'' novel]] {{1st|in book}} *[[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire (abridged audiobook)|''Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire'' abridged audiobook]] *[[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire (junior novelization)|''Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire'' junior novelization]] *''[[X-Wing: The Krytos Trap]]'' {{Ab|X-Wing: The Krytos Trap (unabridged audiobook)}} {{Mo}} ==Sources== *''[[The Secrets of Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire]]'' *''[[Shadows of the Empire Sourcebook]]'' {{CardGameSet|set={{Topps|set=1996 Topps Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire|parent=1}} {{1stp}}|cards= *{{Topps|set=1996 Topps Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire|cardname=Vader Grows Wary of Xizor|link=https://www.rebelscum.com/photo.asp?image=/sote/soteTC020.jpg}} *{{Topps|set=1996 Topps Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire|cardname=Xizor Wants it All|link=https://www.rebelscum.com/photo.asp?image=/sote/soteTC021.jpg}} }} *{{GalaxyCite|9|Filling in the Shadows}} {{Po}} *''[[Star Wars: The Art of the Brothers Hildebrandt]]'' *''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *''[[Star Wars: Behind the Magic]]'' *''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Third Edition, Revised and Expanded]]'' *''[[Coruscant and the Core Worlds]]'' *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} [[Category:Coruscant orbital locations]] [[Category:Galactic Empire locations]] [[Category:New Republic locations]] [[Category:Skyhooks]] [[Category:Space stations in the Core Worlds]]
